compiled_state.title: As a developer, detect-stack.sh recognizes Rails and Jekyll project types
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Extend detect-stack.sh to recognize Rails and Jekyll project types, returning new stack tokens for each.
**Why**: After template installation, da98-0163 re-runs detection. detect-stack.sh currently returns 'unknown' for Rails (no Gemfile detection) and Jekyll. Without recognition, the re-detect step would re-trigger the template menu or require special-casing.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add Rails detection (Gemfile + config/routes.rb marker → new 'ruby-rails' token). Add Jekyll detection (Gemfile + _config.yml marker → new 'ruby-jekyll' token). Tests for both new detection paths.
- OUT: Modifying how other consumers of detect-stack.sh handle these new tokens (that's da98-0163's responsibility).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns 'ruby-rails' for a directory containing Gemfile + config/routes.rb
  ← Satisfies: "re-run Phase 1 auto-detection"
- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns 'ruby-jekyll' for a directory containing Gemfile + _config.yml
  ← Satisfies: "re-run Phase 1 auto-detection"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] New tokens must be documented alongside existing tokens (python-poetry, node-npm, etc.)
- [Testing] Test precedence: what happens if a directory has both config/routes.rb AND _config.yml? (unlikely but should have deterministic behavior)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: detect-stack.sh now recognizes Rails and Jekyll — 2/2 tasks closed, 32 tests pass.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
